entries.title: ON THE SECOND BOOK OF KINGS 4:38 on second-kings 4:42
entries.excerpt: Here two miracles are proposed, which Elisha performed while he was among his disciples. He accomplished the first when he caused death to leave the pot, where, as they said, it had hidden. He performed the second when he nourished one hundred prophets with a little bit of bread. In both miracles he prefigures him who multiplies twice some barley loaves and nourishes with them "about five thousand men, besides women and children."
